body: Foreign investors set a record high in stock buying and selling activities on Monday, November 25, with a total of 10.24 billion shares purchased and 11.91 billion shares sold, resulting in a net foreign volume drop of -1.66 billion shares. The top buys included PT Perusahaan Gas Negara Tbk (PGAS) with 64.04 million shares, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) with 52.32 million shares, and PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) with 145.56 million shares, while the top sells included PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 7.08 billion shares and PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA) with 796.34 million shares.
title: Foreign investors in Jakarta set a record high in stock trading on Monday, with a net sale of 1.66 billion shares.

body: PT Waskita Karya Tbk (WSKT) aims to refocus on its core construction services, including building, dam, and road projects, to improve its financial performance, with 69 ongoing projects worth RP44.4 trillion by October 2024. The company plans to reduce its reliance on government projects, diversify its contract sources, and divest several toll road sections, including the Pemalang-Batang Pejagan-Pemalang toll road in 2025, to improve cash flow and business sustainability.
title: PT Waskita Karya refocuses on core construction services to improve financial performance.

body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) retained its position in the MSCI Indonesia Small Cap Index with a weight of 4.82% and a float-adjusted market cap of $1.33 billion, making it the highest among the 9 constituents. The company's share price has increased 11.64% or 44 points to IDR 422 per share since the beginning of the year, with foreigners buying a net 52.32 million shares on Monday.
title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Attracts Foreign Investors After MSCI Index Rebalancing

body: Individual investor Hapsoro acquired 375.97 million shares of PT Sanurhasta Mitra Tbk (SANUR) through PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk, gaining a 5.73% stake, while Yulisar Khiat increased his holdings in PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk (HEALTH) by 1.40 million shares. Meanwhile, several other investors, including PT Bogamulia Nagadi, PT Startel Communication, and foreign investors Phillip Securities Pte Ltd and OCBC Sec Pte Ltd, made notable transactions in various Indonesian companies, including PT Tempo Scan Pacific Tbk (TSPC), PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk, PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk, PT Paperocks Indonesia Tbk, PT Modernland Realty Tbk, PT Sumber Mas Konstruksi Tbk, and PT Nusa Konstruksi Enjiniring Tbk.
title: Individual investors and companies make notable transactions in Indonesian stock market.

body: PT Bank BTPN Syariah Tbk (BTPS) has seen a 44.91% drop in its stock price since the beginning of the year, from Rp1,670 to Rp920 per share, driven by consistent selling activity from foreign investors, with a net foreign sell of 18.73 million shares last week and 36.23 million shares on Monday. The bank's financial performance has also weakened, with revenue dropping 4.7% year-on-year to Rp4.1 trillion and net profit falling 22.9% to Rp770.6 billion in the first nine months of 2024.
title: Foreign investors sell off PT Bank BTPN Syariah shares amid sharp stock price drop and weakening financial performance.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 414.50 million shares on Friday, November 22, with major buys including PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 404.65 million shares and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with 29.51 million shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors sold a significant number of shares in companies such as PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) with -29.41 million shares and PT Barito Pacific Tbk (BRPT) with -25.40 million shares.
title: Foreign investors buy 5.7 billion shares in Indonesian market, led by acquisition of digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia TBK.

body: PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) plans to issue 1,413,409 new Series C shares through a private placement to fulfill the conversion demand from holders of its mandatory convertible bonds, raising IDR 221.9 million. After the bond conversion, Bumi's issued and paid-up capital will increase to IDR 371.32 billion, comprising 20.77 billion Series A shares, 53.5 billion Series B shares, and 297.05 billion Series C shares.
title: Bumi Resources to issue new shares through private placement to fulfill mandatory convertible bond conversion.

body: Superdry Marine International invests IDR 50 billion in the Halal Industrial Park Sidoarjo, a special economic zone managed by PT Makmur Berkah Amanda Tbk (AMAN), with a total area of 796.65 hectares, aiming to export products to the US, Europe, and Asia. The investment is expected to create new job opportunities for locals and contributes to Indonesia's economic growth, with a target of IDR 97.8 trillion in investments by 2045.
title: Superdry Marine International invests IDR 50 billion in Sidoarjo's Halal Industrial Park to boost exports and create jobs.

body: Buma International, a subsidiary of PT Delta Dunia Makmur Tbk (DOID), will acquire 51% of Dawson Complex, a metallurgical coal mine in Queensland, Australia, for USD 455 million, with completion expected in 2025. The acquisition, funded through internal cash reserves, bank syndicate loan, and guarantee rehabilitation obligation, will reinforce DOID's position in the global metallurgical coal market and promote long-term growth.
title: Buma International to Acquire 51% Stake in Australian Coal Mining Company Dawson Complex for USD 455 Million

body: PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk (UNVR) has signed an agreement to transfer its ice cream business line, PT Magnum Ice Cream Indonesia, for IDR 7 trillion, comprising fixed assets worth IDR 2.55 trillion and inventories worth IDR 1.99 trillion as of September 30, 2024. This transaction, equivalent to approximately 204% of UNVR's equity, is part of Unilever's global plan to spin off its ice cream business, allowing UNVR to focus on its core business.
title: Unilever Indonesia to Divest Ice Cream Business Line to PT Magnum Ice Cream Indonesia in IDR 7 Trillion Deal

body: PT Yulie Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (YULE) has been recognized among the top 50 mid-cap public listed companies for its good corporate governance practices in 2024, receiving the "IICD Corporate Governance Awards Top 50 Mid Cap Emitters 2024" award from the Indonesian Institute of Corporate Directorship. This recognition acknowledges YULE's commitment to professional and compliant corporate governance practices, motivating the company to continuously improve and maintain high standards.
title: PT Yulie Sekuritas Indonesia Recognized for Excellence in Corporate Governance Practices.

body: PT Nusa Raya Cipta Tbk (NRCA) is divesting 46.51 million treasury shares, with a minimum price set at IDR 364 per share, potentially generating IDR 16.93 billion in proceeds. The total projected proceeds from this divestment will be lower than IDR 30.10 billion, which was the price during the final buyback settlement date in 2015.
title: PT Nusa Raya Cipta to Divest 46.51 Million Treasury Shares Starting Next Week

body: PT Wijaya Karya Tbk (WIKA) has injected IDR 18.99 billion into PT Wika Tirta Jaya Jatiluhur (WTJJ) through the issuance of new shares, with the capital injection to be allocated for the construction of the Jatiluhur I regional drinking water supply system. As of September 30, 2024, WIKA has recorded a consolidated net profit of IDR 741.43 billion, with WTJJ contributing less than 1% to the total net profit.
title: Wika Injects IDR 18.99 Billion into Wika Tirta Jaya Jatiluhur for Regional Drinking Water Supply System Project.

body: PT Cikarang Listrindo Tbk (POWR) has secured approval from shareholders to issue foreign currency notes worth USD 500 million, with a maximum yield of 7% per annum and a maturity period of at least 10 years, to improve the company's cash ratio. The proceeds from the notes will be used to pay off bonds maturing in 2026 worth USD 550 million, with the company's current assets standing at USD 556.30 million and total equity at USD 704.12 million as of December 2023.
title: PT Cikarang Listrindo to Issue USD 500 Million Foreign Currency Notes to Improve Cash Ratio

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) has won the Gold Rank Award at the 2024 Asia Sustainability Report Rating event, marking its 4th year of recognition, with a sustainability portfolio reaching IDR 187.6 trillion, or 25.9% of its total credit as of September 2024. This achievement demonstrates BNI's commitment to sustainable finance, with ambitious targets to achieve net zero emission operations by 2028 and net zero emission financing by 2060.
title: BNI Wins Gold Rank Award at 2024 Asia Sustainability Report Rating Event for Fourth Consecutive Year.

body: PT Tower Bersama Infrastructure Tbk (TBIG) is set to issue a shelf-registration bond, Phase IV, with a total principal amount of IDR 2 trillion, as part of its larger public offering program aiming to secure IDR 20 trillion in proceeds. The bond will be offered in two series, with coupons set at 6.45% per annum for a 370-day tenure and 6.75% per annum for a 3-year tenure, and has received an "AA" rating from Fitch Ratings Indonesia.
title: PT Tower Bersama Infrastructure to Issue IDR 2 Trillion Bond as Part of IDR 20 Trillion Public Offering Program

body: Eastspring Investments Indonesia, in collaboration with DBS Indonesia (DBS), has launched the Eastspring ESGQ45 IDX Kehati Index Fund, which primarily invests in securities listed on the ESGQ45 IDX Kehati Index, with a target of IDR 100 billion in assets under management in the first year and IDR 500 billion over the next 3-5 years. The fund, currently available only to institutional investors, aims to cater to aggressive investors with a higher risk tolerance and a medium to long-term investment horizon, with DBS Indonesia noting growing demand for ESG-based mutual funds.
title: Eastspring Investments Indonesia launches ESGQ45 IDX Kehati Index Fund, a new mutual fund focused on environmentally and socially responsible investments.

body: PT Citra Buana Prasida Tbk (CBPE) has injected IDR 85.71 billion into its subsidiary, PT Talaga Ambara Nuansa, which will focus on property development, and the subsidiary's financial statements will be consolidated into CBPE's reports. CBPE's cash and cash equivalents stood at IDR 103.47 billion in September 2024, with total assets reaching IDR 319.04 billion, and revenue surging to IDR 69.66 billion, with an operating profit of IDR 40.26 billion.
title: PT Citra Buana Prasida Injects IDR 85.71 Billion into Subsidiary for Property Development
